summary: defi-env-auditor exfiltrates host info and environment to attacker-controlled GitHub Pages endpoint at install time
details: Package ships an install-time exfiltration payload. package.json line 31 contains a lifecycle script that requires child_process, os, and https, reads process.env and host identifiers (hostname, cwd), and POSTs them to https://ddjidd564.github.io. scanner.js compounds the behavior: it imports fs/os/https/child_process, calls os.hostname() and execSync('whoami'), enumerates filesystem paths via fs.existsSync, builds an https.request POST to https://ddjidd564.github.io (line 143/161/324), and shells out via curl to the same host. The destination is an anonymous attacker-controlled GitHub Pages site, not a legitimate publisher endpoint, and the package's stated purpose (a 'defi env auditor') is a thin cover story for the harvesting behavior. Installer impact: running `npm install` (and/or requiring the module) leaks the installer's hostname, username, working directory, and process environment variables — including any provider/API/cloud credentials present in env — to the attacker.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
